impact: The three blocked items are stale duplicates of accepted narrower recovery work. Moving them out of selectable checkbox form keeps the backlog deterministic, preserves completed tasks, and lets the daemon resume meaningful fixture-first validation work without implementing any PP&D domain behavior or live automation.
summary: Park superseded blocked lifecycle recovery checkboxes so the PP&D daemon can continue from the active narrow validation task instead of treating already-recovered retries as blockers.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 3 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-17: Add a minimal extension-only lifecycle process skeleton fixture with required facts, required documents, user decisions, citation-backed action gates, and explicit no-live-automation notes from existing fixtures only.; Task checkbox-18: Recovery for checkbox-15: add one narrow validation test for the extension-only lifecycle skeleton fixture that checks required facts, required documents, user decisions, citation-backed action gates, and explicit no-live-automation notes without broad lifecycle extraction behavior.; Task checkbox-19: Recovery for checkbox-18: add a one-file exact-schema validation test for `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/extension_lifecycle_process_skeleton.json` and `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/extension_lifecycle_evidence_map.json`; collect committed evidence ids from `sourceInventoryEvidenceIds` and `authoritySources[].evidenceId` in the skeleton, treat evidence-map `categories[].sourceInventoryEvidenceIds` as source-inventory category references only, and do not require the evidence map to expose a top-level evidence-id list.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
